content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

1→= Job Jockey Studbook Data Model
     2→:doctype: book
     3→
     4→// tag::mapping-section[]
     5→//
     6→// Category declarations:
     7→// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)
     8→// jjdsr_: Studbook record
     9→// jjdsm_: Studbook members
    10→// jjdhr_: Heat record
    11→// jjdhm_: Heat members
    12→// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
    13→// jjdpr_: Pace record
    14→// jjdpm_: Pace members
    15→// jjdpe_: Pace enum values (state)
    16→// jjdkr_: Tack record
    17→// jjdkm_: Tack members
    18→// jjdo_:  Operations
    19→// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
    20→//
    21→// Variant patterns:
    22→//   _s: plural form
    23→//   _p: possessive form
    24→//
    25→
    26→// Types
    27→:jjdt_firemark:           <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark>>
    28→:jjdt_firemark_s:         <<jjdt_firemark,Firemarks>>
    29→:jjdt_firemark_p:         <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark's>>
    30→:jjdt_coronet:            <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>
    31→:jjdt_coronet_s:          <<jjdt_coronet,Coronets>>
    32→:jjdt_coronet_p:          <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet's>>
    33→:jjdt_silks:              <<jjdt_silks,Silks>>
    34→:jjdt_favor:              <<jjdt_favor,Favor>>
    35→:jjdt_favor_s:            <<jjdt_favor,Favors>>
    36→
    37→// Studbook record and members
    38→:jjdsr_studbook:          <<jjdsr_studbook,Studbook>>
    39→:jjdsr_studbook_p:        <<jjdsr_studbook,Studbook's>>
    40→:jjdsm_seed:              <<jjdsm_seed,next_heat_seed>>
    41→
    42→// Heat record and members
    43→:jjdhr_heat:              <<jjdhr_heat,Heat>>
    44→:jjdhr_heat_s:            <<jjdhr_heat,Heats>>
    45→:jjdhr_heat_p:            <<jjdhr_heat,Heat's>>
    46→:jjdhm_firemark:          <<jjdhm_firemark,firemark>>
    47→:jjdhm_silks:             <<jjdhm_silks,silks>>
    48→:jjdhm_created:           <<jjdhm_created,creation_time>>
    49→:jjdhm_status:            <<jjdhm_status,status>>
    50→:jjdhm_order:             <<jjdhm_order,order>>
    51→
    52→// Heat enum values
    53→:jjdhe_current:           <<jjdhe_current,current>>
    54→:jjdhe_retired:           <<jjdhe_retired,retired>>
    55→
    56→// Pace record and members
    57→:jjdpr_pace:              <<jjdpr_pace,Pace>>
    58→:jjdpr_pace_s:            <<jjdpr_pace,Paces>>
    59→:jjdpr_pace_p:            <<jjdpr_pace,Pace's>>
    60→:jjdpm_coronet:           <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>
    61→:jjdpm_silks:             <<jjdpm_silks,silks>>
    62→:jjdpm_state:             <<jjdpm_state,state>>
    63→
    64→// Pace enum values
    65→:jjdpe_pending:           <<jjdpe_pending,pending>>
    66→:jjdpe_current:           <<jjdpe_current,current>>
    67→:jjdpe_complete:          <<jjdpe_complete,complete>>
    68→:jjdpe_abandoned:         <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>
    69→
    70→// Tack record and members
    71→:jjdkr_tack:              <<jjdkr_tack,Tack>>
    72→:jjdkr_tack_s:            <<jjdkr_tack,Tacks>>
    73→:jjdkr_tack_p:            <<jjdkr_tack,Tack's>>
    74→:jjdkm_refined:           <<jjdkm_refined,ts>>
    75→:jjdkm_text:              <<jjdkm_text,text>>
    76→
    77→// Operations
    78→:jjdo_nominate:           <<jjdo_nominate,Nominate>>
    79→:jjdo_slate:              <<jjdo_slate,Slate>>
    80→:jjdo_reslate:            <<jjdo_reslate,Reslate>>
    81→:jjdo_rail:               <<jjdo_rail,Rail>>
    82→:jjdo_tally:              <<jjdo_tally,Tally>>
    83→:jjdo_validate:           <<jjdo_validate,Validate>>
    84→:jjdo_heat_exists:        <<jjdo_heat_exists,Heat Exists>>
    85→:jjdo_muster:             <<jjdo_muster,Muster>>
    86→:jjdo_retire_extract:     <<jjdo_retire_extract,Retire Extract>>
    87→:jjdo_current_tack:       <<jjdo_current_tack,Current Tack>>
    88→:jjdo_current_pace:       <<jjdo_current_pace,Current Pace>>
    89→
    90→// Serialization
    91→:jjdz_charset:            <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
    92→:jjdz_encoding:           <<jjdz_encoding,Favor Encoding>>
    93→
    94→// end::mapping-section[]
    95→
    96→== Overview
    97→
    98→The
    99→{jjdsr_studbook}
   100→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   101→It tracks
   102→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   103→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   104→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   105→(discrete actions).
   106→Each
   107→{jjdpr_pace}
   108→maintains a history of
   109→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   110→(plan refinements).
   111→
   112→== Design Principles
   113→
   114→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   115→
   116→All access to the
   117→{jjdsr_studbook}
   118→goes through jjr, the Rust CLI filter.
   119→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   120→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   121→
   122→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   123→The internet is full of bad bash.
   124→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   125→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   126→
   127→=== Git as Journal
   128→
   129→The
   130→{jjdsr_studbook}
   131→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   132→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   133→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   134→
   135→The
   136→{jjdkr_tack}
   137→history within each
   138→{jjdpr_pace}
   139→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   140→Git has the full history, but
   141→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   142→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   143→
   144→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   145→
   146→New
   147→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   148→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   149→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   150→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   151→
   152→The
   153→{jjdhm_order}
   154→array contains full Pace
   155→{jjdt_favor_s}
   156→despite the redundant
   157→{jjdt_firemark}
   158→prefix.
   159→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   160→
   161→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   162→
   163→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and file orchestration.
   164→jjr is a pure filter: stdin JSON in, stdout JSON out.
   165→If jjr crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   166→Bash detects the failure and releases the lock.
   167→
   168→The pattern:
   169→[source,bash]
   170→----
   171→lock_acquire
   172→jjr transform < studbook.json > studbook_new.json || { lock_release; die; }
   173→mv studbook_new.json studbook.json
   174→lock_release
   175→----
   176→
   177→=== Logical vs Serialized Identity
   178→
   179→{jjdt_firemark}
   180→and
   181→{jjdt_coronet}
   182→are the logical identities: simple integers.
   183→{jjdt_favor}
   184→is purely serialization: the base64-encoded, `₣`-prefixed string for JSON keys and human reference.
   185→
   186→This separation keeps the Rust data model clean (integers) while providing readable external identifiers.
   187→Encoding and decoding live in a well-defined serialization layer, not scattered through business logic.
   188→
   189→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   190→
   191→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   192→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   193→
   194→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   195→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   196→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   197→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   198→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity (marking at the hoof line)
   199→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   200→* {jjdt_favor} - external identifier (racing favor/token)
   201→* {jjdsr_studbook} - the registry (breeding record book)
   202→
   203→== Types
   204→
   205→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   206→// ⟦axl_voices axt_number⟧
   207→{jjdt_firemark}::
   208→An integer identifying a
   209→{jjdhr_heat}.
   210→Valid range: 0-4095 (2^12).
   211→Serializes to 2 base64 characters in
   212→{jjdt_favor}
   213→encoding.
   214→
   215→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   216→// ⟦axl_voices axt_number⟧
   217→{jjdt_coronet}::
   218→An integer identifying a
   219→{jjdpr_pace}
   220→within its parent
   221→{jjdhr_heat}.
   222→Valid range: 0-262143 (2^18).
   223→Serializes to 3 base64 characters in
   224→{jjdt_favor}
   225→encoding.
   226→
   227→[[jjdt_silks]]
   228→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   229→{jjdt_silks}::
   230→A kebab-case display name.
   231→Used for human-readable identification of
   232→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   233→and
   234→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   235→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   236→
   237→[[jjdt_favor]]
   238→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   239→{jjdt_favor}::
   240→The serialized form of
   241→{jjdt_firemark}
   242→and
   243→{jjdt_coronet}
   244→used in JSON keys and external references.
   245→Always prefixed with `₣`.
   246→Heat
   247→{jjdt_favor}
   248→is 3 characters (`₣` + encoded
   249→{jjdt_firemark}).
   250→Pace
   251→{jjdt_favor}
   252→is 6 characters (`₣` + encoded
   253→{jjdt_firemark}
   254→+ encoded
   255→{jjdt_coronet}).
   256→
   257→== Records
   258→
   259→=== {jjdsr_studbook}
   260→
   261→[[jjdsr_studbook]]
   262→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   263→{jjdsr_studbook}::
   264→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   265→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjd_studbook.json`.
   266→
   267→==== Members
   268→
   269→* [[jjdsm_seed]]
   270→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   271→{jjdsm_seed}
   272→is the next
   273→{jjdt_firemark}
   274→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   275→Incremented by
   276→{jjdo_nominate}.
   277→
   278→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   279→
   280→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   281→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   282→{jjdhr_heat}::
   283→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   284→Contains zero or more
   285→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   286→representing discrete actions.
   287→Keyed in JSON by Heat
   288→{jjdt_favor}.
   289→
   290→==== Members
   291→
   292→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   293→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   294→{jjdhm_firemark}
   295→is the
   296→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   297→primary key.
   298→Type:
   299→{jjdt_firemark}.
   300→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   301→
   302→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   303→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   304→{jjdhm_silks}
   305→is the
   306→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   307→display name.
   308→Type:
   309→{jjdt_silks}.
   310→
   311→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   312→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   313→{jjdhm_created}
   314→records when the
   315→{jjdhr_heat}
   316→was nominated.
   317→Format: YYMMDD.
   318→
   319→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   320→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   321→{jjdhm_status}
   322→indicates the
   323→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   324→lifecycle state.
   325→Values:
   326→{jjdhe_current}
   327→or
   328→{jjdhe_retired}.
   329→
   330→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   331→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   332→{jjdhm_order}
   333→lists Pace
   334→{jjdt_favor_s}
   335→in execution sequence.
   336→Array of 6-character strings.
   337→May be reordered via
   338→{jjdo_rail}.
   339→
   340→==== Status Values
   341→
   342→[[jjdhe_current]]
   343→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   344→{jjdhe_current}::
   345→The
   346→{jjdhr_heat}
   347→is active and accepting work.
   348→
   349→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   350→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   351→{jjdhe_retired}::
   352→The
   353→{jjdhr_heat}
   354→is complete and archived.
   355→
   356→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   357→
   358→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   359→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   360→{jjdpr_pace}::
   361→A discrete action within a
   362→{jjdhr_heat}.
   363→Contains one or more
   364→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   365→representing plan refinements.
   366→Keyed in JSON by Pace
   367→{jjdt_favor}.
   368→
   369→==== Members
   370→
   371→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   372→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   373→{jjdpm_coronet}
   374→is the
   375→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   376→identity within its parent
   377→{jjdhr_heat}.
   378→Type:
   379→{jjdt_coronet}.
   380→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   381→
   382→* [[jjdpm_silks]]
   383→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   384→{jjdpm_silks}
   385→is the
   386→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   387→display name.
   388→Type:
   389→{jjdt_silks}.
   390→
   391→* [[jjdpm_state]]
   392→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   393→{jjdpm_state}
   394→indicates the
   395→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   396→progress.
   397→Values:
   398→{jjdpe_pending},
   399→{jjdpe_current},
   400→{jjdpe_complete},
   401→or
   402→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   403→
   404→==== State Values
   405→
   406→[[jjdpe_pending]]
   407→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   408→{jjdpe_pending}::
   409→The
   410→{jjdpr_pace}
   411→is queued but not yet started.
   412→
   413→[[jjdpe_current]]
   414→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   415→{jjdpe_current}::
   416→The
   417→{jjdpr_pace}
   418→is actively being worked.
   419→
   420→[[jjdpe_complete]]
   421→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   422→{jjdpe_complete}::
   423→The
   424→{jjdpr_pace}
   425→has been successfully finished.
   426→
   427→[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
   428→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   429→{jjdpe_abandoned}::
   430→The
   431→{jjdpr_pace}
   432→was stopped without completion.
   433→
   434→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   435→
   436→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   437→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   438→{jjdkr_tack}::
   439→A plan refinement entry for a
   440→{jjdpr_pace}.
   441→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   442→form an append-only history.
   443→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   444→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   445→follow.
   446→New
   447→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   448→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   449→
   450→==== Members
   451→
   452→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   453→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   454→{jjdkm_refined}
   455→records when this
   456→{jjdkr_tack}
   457→was added.
   458→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   459→
   460→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   461→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   462→{jjdkm_text}
   463→contains the plan specification.
   464→Non-empty string describing what the
   465→{jjdpr_pace}
   466→should accomplish.
   467→
   468→== Serialization
   469→
   470→[[jjdz_charset]]
   471→{jjdz_charset}::
   472→The 64-character alphabet for
   473→{jjdt_favor}
   474→encoding.
   475→Characters: `A-Za-z0-9` plus two additional characters (TBD: confirm `-_` or other).
   476→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ...).
   477→
   478→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   479→{jjdz_encoding}::
   480→The algorithm for converting between logical identities and
   481→{jjdt_favor}
   482→strings.
   483→+
   484→*Encoding:*
   485→+
   486→. Given
   487→{jjdt_firemark}
   488→F and
   489→{jjdt_coronet}
   490→C
   491→. Encode F as 2 base64 digits: `charset[F/64]` + `charset[F%64]`
   492→. Encode C as 3 base64 digits: `charset[C/4096]` + `charset[(C/64)%64]` + `charset[C%64]`
   493→. Heat
   494→{jjdt_favor}
   495→= `₣` + encoded F
   496→. Pace
   497→{jjdt_favor}
   498→= `₣` + encoded F + encoded C
   499→+
   500→*Decoding:*
   501→+
   502→. Strip `₣` prefix
   503→. For Heat
   504→{jjdt_favor}
   505→(2 chars): F = pos(char1) * 64 + pos(char2)
   506→. For Pace
   507→{jjdt_favor}
   508→(5 chars): extract F from first 2, C from last 3
   509→. C = pos(char3) * 4096 + pos(char4) * 64 + pos(char5)
   510→
   511→== Operations
   512→
   513→Operations are implemented in jjr as pure stdin/stdout filters.
   514→Bash handles file I/O, locking, and orchestration.
   515→
   516→=== Write Operations
   517→
   518→[[jjdo_nominate]]
   519→==== {jjdo_nominate}
   520→
   521→Create a new
   522→{jjdhr_heat}
   523→with empty
   524→{jjdpr_pace}
   525→structure.
   526→
   527→[[jjdo_slate]]
   528→==== {jjdo_slate}
   529→
   530→Add a new
   531→{jjdpr_pace}
   532→to a
   533→{jjdhr_heat}.
   534→
   535→[[jjdo_reslate]]
   536→==== {jjdo_reslate}
   537→
   538→Add a new
   539→{jjdkr_tack}
   540→to a
   541→{jjdpr_pace},
   542→refining the plan.
   543→
   544→[[jjdo_rail]]
   545→==== {jjdo_rail}
   546→
   547→Reorder
   548→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   549→within a
   550→{jjdhr_heat}
   551→by updating
   552→{jjdhm_order}.
   553→
   554→[[jjdo_tally]]
   555→==== {jjdo_tally}
   556→
   557→Update a
   558→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   559→{jjdpm_state}.
   560→
   561→=== Read Operations
   562→
   563→[[jjdo_validate]]
   564→==== {jjdo_validate}
   565→
   566→Check that
   567→{jjdsr_studbook}
   568→JSON conforms to schema.
   569→Returns exit code 0 (valid) or 1 (invalid).
   570→
   571→[[jjdo_heat_exists]]
   572→==== {jjdo_heat_exists}
   573→
   574→Check whether a
   575→{jjdhr_heat}
   576→with given
   577→{jjdt_favor}
   578→exists.
   579→Returns exit code 0 (exists) or 1 (not found).
   580→
   581→[[jjdo_muster]]
   582→==== {jjdo_muster}
   583→
   584→List all
   585→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   586→with summary information.
   587→
   588→[[jjdo_retire_extract]]
   589→==== {jjdo_retire_extract}
   590→
   591→Extract
   592→{jjdhr_heat}
   593→data for archival trophy.
   594→
   595→[[jjdo_current_tack]]
   596→==== {jjdo_current_tack}
   597→
   598→Return the most recent
   599→{jjdkr_tack_p}
   600→{jjdkm_text}
   601→for a given
   602→{jjdpr_pace}.
   603→
   604→[[jjdo_current_pace]]
   605→==== {jjdo_current_pace}
   606→
   607→Return the first
   608→{jjdpr_pace}
   609→in
   610→{jjdhm_order}
   611→whose
   612→{jjdpm_state}
   613→is
   614→{jjdpe_pending}
   615→or
   616→{jjdpe_current}.
   617→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
